payload.description:
# Migration and dual-stack rollout

## Purpose

Ship the new `execute_tactus` MCP tool alongside the existing 64-tool catalog without breaking any existing integration. Establish written sunset criteria so the dual-stack period doesn't become permanent.

## Rollout phases

### Phase 1: Coexist (launch + ~3 months)
- Existing `MCP/plexus_fastmcp_server.py` continues to expose all current tools unchanged.
- New `execute_tactus` lives in a separate MCP server module (or feature flag — TBD), opt-in.
- Users / agents who choose to use it do so deliberately.
- Telemetry on both surfaces from day one: usage frequency, error rate, latency, cost per invocation.

### Phase 2: Recommend new (~3–6 months in)
- Documentation defaults to the new pattern.
- Existing tools marked as legacy in their descriptions.
- Migration cookbook published.
- Existing tools still functional.

### Phase 3: Sunset criteria met (~6+ months in)
- New tool is the default in all surfaces.
- Old tools: deprecation warning on each call.
- Sunset announcement with date.

### Phase 4: Old tools removed (date driven by sunset criteria)
- Old MCP server modules deleted.
- Existing MCP server consolidation epic (separate initiative) cleans up the remaining pieces.

## Sunset criteria (written down so dual-stack doesn't become permanent)

Old tools may be removed when:
- ≥80% of MCP traffic is on the new path for ≥30 consecutive days, AND
- ≥6 months have elapsed since launch, AND
- Migration cookbook covers ≥95% of historical workflow patterns.

## Migration support

- Migration cookbook: per-pattern translation of common 64-tool workflows into Lua snippets. Auto-generated where possible.
- Per-tool deprecation note: when a legacy MCP tool is invoked, response includes a one-line "Lua equivalent: ..." pointer.

## Telemetry from day one

Usage / error / cost / latency data on both surfaces, kept apart so we can measure migration progress and detect regressions.

## Estimate

1 week of focused work, plus ongoing telemetry / cookbook maintenance during the rollout period.
payload.title: Migration and dual-stack rollout
